name: wiki-researcher
description: Conducts multi-turn iterative deep research on specific topics within a codebase with zero tolerance for shallow analysis. Use when the user wants an in-depth investigation, needs to understand how something works across multiple files, or asks for comprehensive analysis of a specific system or pattern.
capabilities: Traces actual code paths and function calls across multiple files
Distinguishes verified facts from inferences with confidence ratings
Maps architectural components and data flow through read-only tools
Identifies design patterns, technical debt, and coupling issues
Creates Mermaid diagrams grounded in real code implementations
Follows 5-iteration research process for comprehensive analysis
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 5 high-risk patterns and 1 medium-risk pattern. All findings are false positives caused by markdown syntax misinterpretation (table pipes, list dashes). The skill is a legitimate codebase research tool with no network requests, file writes, or credential access. It provides structured methodology for deep code analysis using standard read-only tools (Glob, Grep, Read).
valueStatement: Shallow code reviews lead to misunderstandings and bugs. This skill enforces rigorous, evidence-based investigation of codebases through structured multi-turn analysis.
userTitle: Conduct Deep Codebase Research with Evidence-Based Analysis
limitations: Only works with accessible source code files in the workspace
Cannot execute code or test runtime behavior
Requires manual invocation for each research topic
Analysis quality depends on codebase complexity and documentation

useCases.title: Understand Legacy Codebases
useCases.description: New team members rapidly learn complex systems by tracing actual code paths rather than relying on outdated documentation.

useCases.title: Architectural Decision Analysis
useCases.description: Evaluate design patterns, coupling, and technical debt before refactoring or adding new features.

useCases.title: Bug Investigation
useCases.description: Trace data flow and function calls to identify root causes of complex bugs spanning multiple modules.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Exploration
promptTemplates.prompt: How does authentication work in this codebase?
promptTemplates.scenario: New developer needs to understand auth flow

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Mapping
promptTemplates.prompt: Map the data flow from API entry point to database storage for user creation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Planning a refactor of user registration

promptTemplates.title: Pattern Investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: What design patterns are used in the payment processing module? Identify trade-offs and risks.
promptTemplates.scenario: Evaluating technical debt in critical systems

promptTemplates.title: Deep System Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Trace the complete request lifecycle from ingress through middleware, services, and data layers. Include all transformations, error handling, and external integrations.

bestPractices: Provide specific topics or questions rather than generic 'analyze everything' requests
Use when you need depth rather than quick answers - this skill prioritizes thoroughness over speed
Combine with file navigation tools to jump to specific code locations mentioned in findings
Request Mermaid diagrams when visualizing complex architectures or data flows
antiPatterns: Avoid using for simple grep searches - use basic file search instead
Do not expect runtime behavior analysis - this skill traces static code paths only
Avoid asking about files outside the accessible workspace directory
Do not use for time-sensitive tasks - the 5-iteration process requires multiple tool calls

faq.question: How is this different from normal code analysis?
faq.answer: This skill enforces rigorous evidence standards. Every claim must cite file paths and line numbers. It uses 5 research iterations and explicitly distinguishes facts from inferences with confidence ratings.

faq.question: Can it execute code or test functionality?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads and analyzes source code. It cannot execute code, run tests, or observe runtime behavior.

faq.question: How long does analysis take?
faq.answer: The skill performs 5 iterative passes over the codebase. Complex topics may require multiple tool calls across dozens of files. Expect thorough analysis to take longer than casual exploration.

faq.question: What tools does it use?
faq.answer: Only read-only tools: Glob for file discovery, Grep for content search, and Read for file reading. It never writes files or executes commands.

faq.question: Will it work on any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill traces code paths and structure, not specific language syntax. It works with any codebase where files can be read as text.

faq.question: Can it find bugs or security issues?
faq.answer: It can identify suspicious patterns and logic flaws by tracing code paths. However, it is not a replacement for dedicated security scanners or dynamic analysis tools.
